commands.review_summary:
Adds opt-in iMessage catchup config/schema, cursor persistence, history replay bridge wiring, longer echo-cache retention, docs/changelog, generated config metadata, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source/docs show the iMessage downtime-catchup gap, and the previously reported PR-branch cap bug is source-reproducible with more valid rows than `perRunLimit`; I did not run tests because this pass is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The automated finding appears fixed; remaining work is protected maintainer review plus final CI for an XL feature/config PR, not a narrow repair job.

target.title: feat(imessage): inbound catchup (cursor + replay loop + monitor wiring)
target.author: omarshahine

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: omarshahine
commands.command: finding addressed in `6c795ff98f`. **root cause:** the parse-rejected high-watermark fix in `8c6421b846` introduced the regression — `rawwatermarkrowid` covered every raw row the fetcher saw, so when `collected.length > perrunlimit` the dropped tail's rowids leaked into the watermark and the catchup loop applied them as a cursor floor. **fix:** in `catchup-bridge.ts`, after sorting + slicing to `capped`, clamp `effectivewatermarkrowid` / `effectivewatermarkms` to `capped[capped.length-1]` when truncation hits. when no truncation, watermark still covers parse-rejected rows interspersed with the dispatched batch (the original forward-progress guarantee). when `capped.length === 0` (pathological limit=0), suppress the watermark entirely. **regression coverage:** extended the existing `caps cross-chat results at perrunlimit, oldest first` test to assert `summary.cursorafter.lastseenrowid === 200` (the last dispatched rowid), not 203 (the raw watermark of the chat-2 tail). without the fix this assertion fails with `expected 203 to be 200`. **acceptance criteria all green:** - `pnpm vitest run extensions/imessage/src/monitor/catchup.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/catchup-bridge.test.ts` → 29/29 pass - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` → all matched files use the correct format - `pnpm lint:extensions:bundled` → 0 warnings, 0 errors
commands.freeform_prompt: finding addressed in `6c795ff98f`. **Root cause:** the parse-rejected high-watermark fix in `8c6421b846` introduced the regression — `rawWatermarkRowid` covered every raw row the fetcher saw, so when `collected.length > perRunLimit` the dropped tail's rowids leaked into the watermark and the catchup loop applied them as a cursor floor. **Fix:** in `catchup-bridge.ts`, after sorting + slicing to `capped`, clamp `effectiveWatermarkRowid` / `effectiveWatermarkMs` to `capped[capped.length-1]` when truncation hits. When no truncation, watermark still covers parse-rejected rows interspersed with the dispatched batch (the original forward-progress guarantee). When `capped.length === 0` (pathological limit=0), suppress the watermark entirely. **Regression coverage:** extended the existing `caps cross-chat results at perRunLimit, oldest first` test to assert `summary.cursorAfter.lastSeenRowid === 200` (the last dispatched rowid), not 203 (the raw watermark of the chat-2 tail). Without the fix this assertion fails with `expected 203 to be 200`. **Acceptance criteria all green:** - `pnpm vitest run extensions/imessage/src/monitor/catchup.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/catchup-bridge.test.ts` → 29/29 pass - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` → All matched files use the correct format - `pnpm lint:extensions:bundled` → 0 warnings, 0 errors
